chooser.title: How would you like to start?

fromScratch.title: Start from scratch
fromScratch.description: Empty workspace. Useful when you already know which rules you want to model.

template.title: Use a template
template.description: Start from a ready-made example (engagement, reward on completion, speed bonus...).

extend.title: Extend an existing strategy
extend.description: Wrap a built-in (e.g. default) with pre/post rules without rewriting it.

autosave.error: Autosave failed - use Save to retry.

search.hint: Click a result to add it to the canvas.

lifecycle.publishBody: Publishing makes v{{version}} the live version assigned to games and tasks. Any previously published version of "{{name}}" is archived automatically.
lifecycle.archiveBody: Archiving takes the strategy out of circulation: it can no longer be edited or published, though its history stays available.

simulate.title: Test strategy
simulate.intro: Run your strategy with fictitious data to see how many points it would award. Set the accumulated values it reads (times submitted, timings, others' activity) below. Nothing is persisted to production.
simulate.dataHint: Extra data your app would send when awarding points. Leave it as {} if you don't use data.<key> fields.
simulate.runsHint: We run the strategy this many times in a row, adding the "+ per submission" step to each accumulated value so you can see how points evolve.
simulate.fieldsHint: These are the values your rules depend on. Set a starting value for each.
simulate.noFields: This strategy doesn't read any accumulated values - just fill the test data above and run it.

fields.user.measurements_count: Times this user already submitted the task
fields.task.measurements_count: Total submissions for the task (all users)
fields.user.recent_measurements_count: User's submissions in the last 5 minutes
fields.user.new_last_window_diff: New time diff vs. last window (s)

simulate.mockHint: Override any field with a fixed value, e.g. {"task.measurements_count": 1}. Merged on top of the inputs above; useful for data.<key> overrides.

validation.RULE_THEN_EMPTY: The rule needs at least one action. Drag a block from the 'Assign' category (e.g. 'assign points') and snap it into the 'then' slot.
validation.RULE_WHEN_REQUIRED: The rule needs a condition. Connect a block into the 'when' slot.
validation.PROGRAM_NO_RULES: Add at least one rule to the canvas before saving or testing.
validation.FIELD_PATH_NOT_ALLOWED: Field '{{path}}' is not allowed. Pick a field from the dropdown or use 'data.<key>'.

parentSchema.title: Parent: {{name}}
parentSchema.name: Name
parentSchema.noVariables: This strategy exposes no editable variables.
parentSchema.hint: Use the "Parent overrides" toolbox category to tweak these values.

alerts.loadFailed: Could not load the strategy into the editor (corrupt data): {{error}}.
alerts.noParent: Select a parent strategy before saving.
alerts.noParentSim: Select a parent strategy before testing.
alerts.notAStrategyBundle: The file does not look like a strategy bundle: missing astJson.
alerts.publishSuccess: Strategy published (v{{version}}). It is now the live version.
alerts.unknownError: Unknown error when contacting the backend.

steps.welcome: Welcome to the strategy editor. Here you can design point rules without writing code.
steps.name: Give your strategy a clear name - you'll see it when assigning it to a game or task.
steps.mode: Pick whether you start from scratch or extend an existing strategy.
steps.workspace: Drag blocks from the side panel onto this canvas. Snap them together like puzzle pieces to form rules.
steps.simulate: When you have a rule, test it here with a fictitious user and task. Set the accumulated values it reads, or switch to "several submissions" to watch points evolve - nothing is persisted to production.
steps.save: Save as draft as often as you like. It only reaches production when an admin publishes the version.
steps.history: Every save creates a version. From here you can view history and roll back.
